Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.terms

Class Term

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Hashable
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.terms.Term
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<Term>
Direct Known Subclasses:
AssociativeTerm, SimpleTerm
public abstract class Term extends Hashable implements Comparable<Term>
Abstract base class for terms.

    Method
    Description
    abstract void
    collectLocalVariables(Set<LocalVariable> localVariables)
    Adds to the specified set all instances of LocalVariable occurring in this term.

    Adds to the specified set all instances of Variable occurring in this term.

    Returns true if this term contains the specified term and false otherwise.

  • Method Details

    • getSort

      public Sort getSort()
      Returns the sort of this term.
      Returns:
      the sort of this term

    • isConstant

      public abstract boolean isConstant()
      Returns true if this term does not contain any variables. Returns false otherwise.
      Returns:
      true if this term does not contain any variables. Returns false otherwise

    • isQuantified

      public abstract boolean isQuantified()
      Return true if this term contains at least one local variables. Returns false otherwise.
      Returns:
      true if this term contains at least one local variables. Returns false otherwise

    • isForall

      public abstract boolean isForall()
      Returns true if isQuantified() returns true and if the local variables are universally quantified.

      By construction, a term cannot contain both universally and existentially quantified variables at the same time.

      Returns:
      true if isQuantified() returns true and if the local variables are universally quantified.

    • getInverse

      public static <T extends Term> T getInverse(T term)
      Returns the inverse of a term. The inverse is taken by replacing all universally quantified variables by an existentially quantified variable and vice-versa.
      Type Parameters:
      T -
      Parameters:
      term - the term to inverse
      Returns:
      the inversed term

    • substituteSimpleTerms

      public static <T extends Term> T substituteSimpleTerms(Map<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map, T term)
      Substitutes variables and local variables in the term according to the specified map.
      Type Parameters:
      T -
      Parameters:
      map - the substitution map
      term - the term on which to apply the substitution
      Returns:
      the result of this substitution

    • toString

      public abstract String toString(HashMap<Variable,String> variableMap)

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • contains

      public abstract boolean contains(SimpleTerm term)
      Returns true if this term contains the specified term and false otherwise.
      Parameters:
      term - the term to be looked for
      Returns:
      true if this term contains the specified term and false otherwise.

    • collectVariables

      public abstract void collectVariables(Set<Variable> variables)
      Adds to the specified set all instances of Variable occurring in this term.
      Parameters:
      variables - the set to which the instances must be added

    • collectLocalVariables

      public abstract void collectLocalVariables(Set<LocalVariable> localVariables)
      Adds to the specified set all instances of LocalVariable occurring in this term.
      Parameters:
      localVariables - the set to which the instances must be added

    • equalsWithDifferentVariables

      public abstract boolean equalsWithDifferentVariables(Term term, HashMap<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map)
      Returns true if this term is equal to the specified term when applying the substitution given in the specified map. Beside, all variables not already present in the map are added to the map while computing the equality if the terms match. For instance, x$1.equalsWithDifferentVariables(x$2, map) with map.isEmpty()==true will return true and the map will contain the entry {x$1,x$2}. Calling x$1.equalsWithDifferentVariables(x$2, map) with map containing the entry {x$1,x$3} will return false and the map will remain unchanged.
      Parameters:
      term - the term to be checked for equality
      map - the correspondance map for the variables in the terms
      Returns:
      true if the terms are equal, false otherwise
